title: Podman run hangs with userns=keep-id
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I have been using the visual studio code's remote containers plugin along with podman rootless containers very happily on my laptop, however now I installed Arch Linux on my desktop, set up the same environment and the containers created by vscode seem to hang.

It builds the devcontainer well, but then it just hangs forever upon creation with this command:

```
[3836 ms] Start: Run: /usr/bin/podman run --sig-proxy=false -a STDOUT -a STDERR --mount source=/home/username/Build/C#/container-test,target=/workspace,type=bind,Z --mount type=volume,src=vscode,dst=/vscode -l vsch.local.folder=/home/username/Build/C#/container-test -l vsch.quality=stable -l vsch.remote.devPort=0 -u vscode --userns=keep-id --entrypoint /bin/sh vsc-container-test-f38c80e81c2075afa91d75879a3c6e3a-uid -c echo Container started
```

I tried to run this command manually from terminal and it hangs there as well. However if I remove the `--userns=keep-id` flag it launches and seemingly works well (though then I don't have permission to access most of the files).

I thought there might be something about my setup so I wanted to see if the same happens with a simpler setup: `podman run --rm -it --name=test --userns=keep-id alpine:latest`. And surprisingly this one works well with the same flag and doesn't hang.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install `vscode` and its remote containers extension, specify `podman` as their docker path.

2. Create any devcontainer (I tried with C# and Python), add the following lines to `devcontainer.json`:
```
	"workspaceMount": "source=${localWorkspaceFolder},target=/workspace,type=bind,Z",
	"workspaceFolder": "/workspace",

	"runArgs": ["--userns=keep-id"],
	"containerUser": "vscode"
```

3. Rebuild and reopen the container and wait until it hangs.

**Describe the results you received:**

The run command fails to succeed.

**Describe the results you expected:**

`podman run` works.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.4
Git Commit:   f6526ada1025c2e3f88745ba83b8b461ca659933
Built:        Thu Dec  9 19:30:40 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  cpus: 4

      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
Name            : podman

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
AppArmor is enabled on the system, but I tried without as well, it hung.

comments.body: Is there a potential that the app in the container is failing when `keep-id` is specified? I'm not familiar with the image, but if you run something basic like `sh` in it instead of the configured entrypoint, does it work?
Try to run it with the fedora image, if that works then the issue is probably with the image.

These are the results I am getting if I change the entrypoint: 
```
[username@todo-pc Downloads]$ podman run --sig-proxy=false -a STDOUT -a STDERR --mount source=/home/username/Build/C#/container-test,target=/workspace,type=bind,Z --mount type=volume,src=vscode,dst=/vscode -l vsch.local.folder=/home/username/Build/C#/container-test -l vsch.quality=stable -l vsch.remote.devPort=0 -u vscode --userns=keep-id --entrypoint /bin/echo vsc-container-test-f38c80e81c2075afa91d75879a3c6e3a-uid /bin/sh
^CError: error creating container storage: error creating an ID-mapped copy of layer "2483cf94452c8d334610582254ea539062aa228209a1764e17f97e9d5b94d10e": signal: interrupt
[username@todo-pc Downloads]$ podman run --sig-proxy=false -a STDOUT -a STDERR --mount source=/home/username/Build/C#/container-test,target=/workspace,type=bind,Z --mount type=volume,src=vscode,dst=/vscode -l vsch.local.folder=/home/username/Build/C#/container-test -l vsch.quality=stable -l vsch.remote.devPort=0 -u vscode --entrypoint /bin/echo vsc-container-test-f38c80e81c2075afa91d75879a3c6e3a-uid /bin/sh
/bin/sh
```

Fedora hangs too:

```
[username@todo-pc Downloads]$ podman run --rm -it --name=test --userns=keep-id fedora:latest /bin/echo hi
^CError: error creating container storage: error creating an ID-mapped copy of layer "389adea752cd55355cae09d2b3038c0b155e992a0d32fcecb3d281abf6a8c5cf": signal: interrupt
[username@todo-pc Downloads]$ podman run --rm -it --name=test fedora:latest /bin/echo hi
hi
```

can it be that it is just slow to do the chown?

Could you try with something like busybox?
haven't got any feedback, closing.  Please reopen if it still not working for you.

It's certainly not an issue with chowning, I can see that those processes are constantly respawned for some reason in htop and the container never starts up. I could reproduce this with a fresh Fedora installation as well.

And I can't reopen the issue. ☺️

Reopened.

Please run the same command with `--log-level debug` and attach the output here.

Could you please share more information on what processes are respawned?

I uploaded the output here: https://pastebin.com/raw/SPX9n5Sh

And my bad, its not respawned, but hanging:
```
username      20619  8.6  0.2 1796504 48412 pts/1   Sl+  16:33   0:00 storage-chown-by-maps /home/username/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/87099851679d356af602383953860e39996969d72e3795f39802d7b9bbecabcf/merged
```

the ` storage-chown-by-maps ` process is chowning the files.

Could you please run strace (`strace -vv -s 10000 -p $PID_OF_storage-chown-by-maps`) on that process when it hangs to see what it is doing?

It might depend on the image you are using

There it is: https://pastebin.com/raw/PTqe6RHN

I saw its going thru files so I let it run for a long time and after around 20 minutes it started up. I assume it's just really slow for some reason and I don't see why. Thanks for the help though, looks like it's not an issue with podman.

I am experiencing the same issue on RHEL 9 with up-to-date podman. Running with default --userns starts the container immediately, but keep-id hangs the `podman run`.

@Diniboy1123 have you ever found a root cause to this issue?

Once you do keep-id on a single image, it should chown the image to match the new UIDMap after the initial creation of the image, then all future containers that use this image in the --userns=keep-id, should be as fast as --userness=""

Well, this is still bothering me - @rhatdan starting of a container takes 20 minutes, but then every single `podman` command executes under unprivileged user takes another 20 minutes. So whenever I want to remove the container or simply execute `podman ps` Im waiting for 20 minutes. This is not the case for root user. Any more ideas here? :)

```
Red Hat Enterprise Linux release 9.2 (Plow)
podman version 4.4.1
```

When I attach to the podman process when executing for example podman ps I see the following:
```
futex(0x5563d6448508, FUTEX_WAIT_PRIVATE, 0, NULL
```
Please open a new issue, does not sound like you have the same problem unless the number of files in your container image is huge.
